title: Career Interest Test
description: Rate 48 work activities to find your strongest RIASEC-style career interest pattern.
instructions: Rate how much you would enjoy each activity, not how skilled you already are.
Answer for your real preferences, not what sounds impressive.
Use the result as a career exploration map, not a fixed career prescription.

scaleQuestions.question: Repairing a device, machine, or physical object.
Working outdoors or in a hands-on environment.
Using tools, equipment, or technical instruments.
Building a prototype to test whether an idea works.
Solving practical problems with your hands.
Operating hardware, vehicles, or production equipment.
Improving a physical workflow for safety or speed.
Learning a technical craft through repeated practice.
Investigating why a system behaves the way it does.
Analyzing data to find patterns or causes.
Reading deeply about science, technology, or ideas.
Designing an experiment to test a hypothesis.
Solving abstract or technical puzzles.
Explaining a complex topic with evidence.
Comparing theories before choosing a conclusion.
Studying a problem independently until it makes sense.
Creating visual, written, musical, or digital work.
Developing original concepts for a campaign or story.
Improving how something feels, sounds, or looks.
Working in a flexible role with room for self-expression.
Brainstorming many unusual approaches to a brief.
Using design, language, or style to influence emotion.
Producing content that carries a distinct point of view.
Reworking an idea until it feels aesthetically right.
Teaching someone a skill step by step.
Helping people work through a personal challenge.
Facilitating a group conversation or workshop.
Coaching a teammate toward better performance.
Designing services around user or community needs.
Creating a supportive environment for others.
Mediating tension between people with different needs.
Working directly with clients, students, patients, or members.
Pitching an idea and persuading others to support it.
Leading a team toward an ambitious target.
Negotiating a deal, partnership, or agreement.
Starting a project when the outcome is uncertain.
Making decisions that affect revenue or growth.
Representing a product, cause, or team publicly.
Competing to win a measurable result.
Turning a rough opportunity into a concrete plan.
Organizing records, documents, or structured information.
Checking work carefully for accuracy and compliance.
Creating repeatable processes that reduce mistakes.
Managing schedules, budgets, or operational details.
Working with spreadsheets, systems, or databases.
Following clear standards to produce reliable output.
Improving a workflow through documentation.
Maintaining order in a busy team or operation.

REALISTIC.title: Realistic Builder
REALISTIC.summary: You are drawn to tangible work, tools, systems, and practical problem solving.
weaknesses: May lose patience with vague strategy
relationships: Clear tasks, physical or technical output, autonomy, and practical feedback.
growthPlan: Compare technical roles by daily tasks
Document your process to make your skill visible

INVESTIGATIVE.title: Investigative Analyst
INVESTIGATIVE.summary: You enjoy research, analysis, evidence, and solving complex questions.
weaknesses: Can struggle with repetitive people-facing work
relationships: Independent thinking time, intellectual challenge, and respect for evidence.

ARTISTIC.title: Artistic Creator
ARTISTIC.summary: You are motivated by originality, expression, aesthetics, and flexible creation.
relationships: Flexibility, creative ownership, visual or narrative work, and room for taste.
growthPlan: Learn how creative work is evaluated

SOCIAL.title: Social Helper
SOCIAL.summary: You are energized by helping, teaching, coaching, and improving human outcomes.
weaknesses: May avoid analytical or solitary work
relationships: People contact, visible impact, collaborative culture, and meaningful service.
growthPlan: Clarify the population you want to serve
Track measurable outcomes of your support

ENTERPRISING.title: Enterprising Leader
ENTERPRISING.summary: You like influence, initiative, persuasion, and turning opportunities into results.
weaknesses: May move faster than details support
relationships: Targets, autonomy, visible stakes, and space to influence decisions.

CONVENTIONAL.title: Conventional Organizer
CONVENTIONAL.summary: You prefer structure, accuracy, systems, and reliable execution.
relationships: Clear standards, predictable workflows, accountability, and stable expectations.
growthPlan: Look for roles with process ownership
